taxonomy: Ptycanthera oblongata is classified as a species within the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This classification places Ptycanthera oblongata alongside other sedges, which are perennial plants typically found in wetland environments. The family Cyperaceae is further grouped within the order Poales, which includes various monocot families. The order Poales is part of the class Liliopsida, a group of flowering plants. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other monocots.
